summaryTemplate: {{className}} is the {{patternRole}}. It interns instances in a pool keyed by intrinsic state and hands out shared references, so many fine-grained objects cost little memory. Pattern body lives at {{file}}:{{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}.
chosenRationaleTemplate: Tagged as Flyweight because {{className}} pairs a shared-instance accessor with a keyed pool (lines {{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}) and returns shared ownership — the interning shape.

flyweight_factory_method.note: The shared accessor. It returns an existing instance from the pool when one exists instead of allocating a duplicate.

flyweight_shared_pool.note: The shared pool. Keying instances by intrinsic state is what enables safe sharing.

runnerUpComparisonTemplates.Factory Method: Factory Method hides which concrete product is built. Flyweight's factory additionally reuses already-built instances from a pool — the sharing/interning is the point, not just construction.
